readme.current_age: Simulation start age. Year 1 = this age + 1. All age-gated logic (RMD start, Roth conversion window, tira_age_gate) is relative to this. Set to "compute" to derive automatically from birth_year (and optionally birth_month + birth_day). Example: "current_age": "compute"
readme.birth_year: Used to determine SECURE 2.0 RMD start age: born 1950 or earlier = age 72, born 1951-1959 = age 73, born 1960 or later = age 75. Does not drive simulation year counting.
readme.assumed_death_age: Last simulation year. Simulation runs from current_age+1 to this age inclusive.
readme.filing_status: Options: 'MFJ' (married filing jointly), 'single', 'MFS' (married filing separately), 'HOH' (head of household). Drives federal and state tax bracket selection.
readme.spouse: Optional. Include only if filing_status is MFJ. sole_beneficiary_for_ira: true enables spousal rollover RMD rules (spouse uses their own life expectancy after inheriting).

beneficiaries.primary: First to inherit. share_percent values must sum to 100.
beneficiaries.contingent: Inherit only if all primary beneficiaries predecease. share_percent must sum to 100.
beneficiaries.eligible_designated_beneficiary: true = can use own life expectancy for inherited IRA RMDs (applies to: surviving spouse, disabled/chronically ill, minor child until majority, beneficiary within 10 years of owner's age). false = 10-year rule applies — full account must be distributed within 10 years.
beneficiaries.per_stirpes: true = if this beneficiary predeceases, their share passes to their descendants rather than being redistributed to surviving beneficiaries.

rmd_policy.extra_handling_options: What to do with RMD amounts above what is needed for living expenses. 'reinvest_in_brokerage' = park surplus in taxable brokerage (still taxable as ordinary income that year, but then invested). 'spend' = treat as extra cash income. 'hold_cash' = keep as uninvested cash reserve.

roth_conversion_policy.enabled: true/false. Master switch for all Roth conversions.
roth_conversion_policy.window_years: List of year ranges when conversions are allowed. E.g. ['now-75', '1-10']. 'now' = current_age. Conversions only fire within these windows — typically the gap between retirement and RMD start is the prime window.
roth_conversion_policy.keepit_below_max_marginal_fed_rate_optio: 'fill the bracket' = convert up to the top of the current bracket (maximizes conversion without jumping to next rate). A rate string like '22%' = stop before hitting that marginal rate. 'none' = no rate cap.
roth_conversion_policy.avoid_niit: true = halt conversion if it would push net investment income above the $250k NIIT threshold, avoiding the 3.8% surcharge.
roth_conversion_policy.rmd_assist_options: 'convert' = count pending RMD toward Roth conversion room (avoids double-counting income). 'none' = treat RMD as independent of conversion.
roth_conversion_policy.tax_payment_source_options: 'BROKERAGE' = pay the conversion tax bill from your brokerage account — your take-home withdrawal is NOT affected, but brokerage balance decreases. 'withhold' = deduct tax from the conversion amount before crediting Roth — reduces the Roth benefit but preserves brokerage.
roth_conversion_policy.irmaa_guard: enabled: true = cap conversion to avoid crossing an IRMAA Medicare premium tier. IRMAA tiers are based on income 2 years prior — relevant for ages 65+.

readme.state: Resident state for state income tax bracket selection. Must match a state name exactly as keyed in src/config/taxes_states_mfj_single.json (e.g. 'California', 'New York', 'Texas'). Drives which state brackets are loaded by load_tax_unified(). Can be overridden at runtime via the Run panel without changing this file.
readme.rmd_table: Which IRS RMD table to use. Options: 'uniform_lifetime' (default — applies to all account owners), 'joint_survivor' (use only if your spouse is your sole IRA beneficiary AND is more than 10 years younger — produces lower RMDs). The system file src/config/rmd.json contains the Uniform Lifetime Table. Joint Survivor table must be added to src/config/ before selecting this option.
readme.runtime_overrides_note: Fields 'state', 'filing_status', and 'rmd_table' can be overridden at runtime from the Run panel without editing this file. Runtime overrides are ephemeral — they apply to that run only and are recorded in the run's result metadata so you always know which parameters produced which results.
readme.target_age: Final simulation age (inclusive). n_years = target_age - current_age. Clamped to 10-60 year range. Replaces assumed_death_age for simulation length control.
